para_trans: Forest Land-Pune
Re-Diversion of 0.06845 ha. Forest land for
laying of underground optical fiber cable
along with the road in RoW of Existing Road
from Kondwa (Kh) to Mohammadwadi
Village (NIBM Road) in Taluka-Haveli,
District- Pune in the State of Maharashtra.
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department
Government Order No.: FLD-1926/CR.06/F-10
Mantralaya, Mumbai-400 032
Reference:
Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests & Nodal Officer, MS, Nagpur Letter No.
Preamble:-
Order:-
In exercise of powers under general approval given by MoEF&CC, Govt. of India vide Handbook of VSESA Guidelines-2023, Chapter 4 (para-4.2), Government of Maharashtra is
pleased to grant 'In-principle' approval to the re-diversion of 0.06845 ha. of Forest land under
Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Adhiniyam, 1980 in favour of Bharati Airtel Ltd., Pune for
laying of underground optical fiber cable along with the road in RoW of Existing Road from
Kondwa (Kh) to Mohammadwadi Village (NIBM Road) in Taluka-Haveli, District- Pune in the
State of Maharashtra.
2. The details of Forest Land proposed for diversion are as under:-
table_trans: Bharati Airtel Ltd., Pune has submitted a proposal for re-diversion of 0.06845 ha. of Forest
land for laying of underground optical fiber cable along with the road in RoW of Existing Road
from Kondwa (Kh) to Mohammadwadi Village (NIBM Road) in Taluka-Haveli, District- Pune in
the State of Maharashtra to the State Forest Department. APCCF & Nodal Officer has submitted
this proposal to Government of Maharashtra vide letter under referred above.
Laying of underground Optical Fiber Cable along the road in RoW of existing road from Kondhwa (Kh) to Mohammadwadi_ Village (NIBM Road).
Total forest land proposed for re-diversion

para_trans: This 'In-principle' approval is issued subject to the following conditions-
1) The legal status of the land shall remain unchanged.
2) User Agency shall defray the cost of plantation (including ten years maintenance) of 100
tree shall be levied from the User Agency towards Compensatory Afforestation. As far as
possible, a mixture of local indigenous species along with 10% RET species of Pune
District shall be planted and monoculture of any species may be avoided. Details of land
proposed for Plantation (Record of Rights & KML File), shall be submitted before Stage-
II/Final approval.
3) User Agency shall submit NOC of Public Work Department along with Stage-1
compliance.
4) The Optical Fiber Cable Line will be laid within maximum size of the trench of 2 meter
depth & 0.5 meter width with cable line under the OFC pipeline.
5) If User Agency has not submitted bank guarantee or paid security deposit with concern
road authority (NHAI/MSRDC/PWD/ZP), the agency shall submit a bank guarantee at the
rate of Rs.50/- per running meter and an undertaking to that effect shall also be submitted.
If the agency fails to reclaim the land to the satisfaction of concerned Deputy Conservator
of Forests, the cost for reclamation will be covered from the bank guarantee.
6) If the cable is going to be laid in the formation width of the road, then additional charges
in form of bank guarantee shall be given at the rate of Rs.35/- per running meter. Similarly
for reclamation of side drain and for tarred portion, the guarantee will be at the rate of
Rs.300/- per meter and Rs.750/- per meter respectively. An undertaking shall be obtained
from the User Agency that it has agreed to make good any loss to Forest/ Environment
while laying the cable in the forest area and during maintenance of the same in future. For
further maintenance of the project, the agency shall obtain permission from the concerned
Deputy Conservator of Forests as and when required.
1) The forest land shall not be used for any purpose other than specified in the proposal.
8) No felling of trees shall be done during the implementation of project.
9) No new forest area beyond the Right of Way of existing road should be broken for laying
Optical Fiber Cable.
10) The User Agency shall pay the cost of Net Present Value, if required in future.
11) Underground Optical Fiber Cable can be laid only outside the confines of the Protected
Areas.
12) No labour camp shall be established on the forest land.
13) After completion of the project, User Agency will reclaim the area under RoW suitably.
14) The layout plan of the proposal shall not be changed without prior approval of the State
Government.
15) This sanction is subject to the final clearance by the Department of Information &
Technology for the project and concerned road authorities for the project.
16) The User Agency shall compensate the private owners of forest land if any, in accordance
with the prevalent laws, rules & guidelines.
17) The User Agency shall obtain Environmental Clearance as per the provisions of the
Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
18) All other conditions as may be mandatory under relevant Acts, Rules and Guidelines shall
be complied by the User Agency.
19) All the conditions stipulated by the Forest Department shall be binding on User Agency.
20) The User Agency shall make good of any loss to Forest / Environment.
21) In case, the proposed area falls in the RoW of the road passing through National Parks and
Wildlife Sanctuaries, prior permission of the State Board for Wildlife and Chief Wildlife
Warden should be obtained as per relevant guidelines in this regard.
22) All necessary approvals required for the project as per the prevailing laws of the land would
be obtained by the User Agency.
23) Any breach of the conditions by the User Agency will be treated as violation of the
provisions of the Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Adhiniyam, 1980.
This Government Order is available at the Government of Maharashtra's website.
www.imaharashtra.gov.in with reference No. 202603111520116119. This Order has been signed
digitally. By order and in the name of the Governor of Maharashtra.
(Sanjay Jadhav)
Under Secretary to the Government of Maharashtra
